title: YB-MIDL Config Document
description: A complete boat-dashboard definition: a versioned set of screens, each laying out data-bound elements, plus optional global settings, defaults, presets and alarms. This schema checks document STRUCTURE (shape and types); internal meaning (reference resolution, unique ids, layout arithmetic) is enforced by a separate semantic pass, and device fit by capability satisfaction.

midl.description: MIDL grammar version this document targets, as MAJOR.MINOR.BUILD (e.g. "1.0.0"). The major must match the device manifest's major for the document to be admissible.

settings.description: Optional document-wide settings (theme, units, refresh rate, …). Free-form object interpreted by the renderer.

defaults.description: Optional default element attributes inherited by elements that do not override them. Free-form object.

screens.description: Ordered list of dashboard screens. A document must define at least one screen; the user pages between them on the device.

alarms.description: Optional threshold alarms evaluated against data sources. Each alarm fires at its level when its source crosses a lt/gt bound.

presets.description: Optional document-local named layout presets (in addition to the built-in registry). Free-form object.

screen.description: One dashboard page: a named set of elements and a layout tree that arranges them. May carry per-device-class variant layouts.

id.description: Unique screen identifier (must be unique across the document; used for navigation and addressing).

title.description: Optional human-readable screen title shown in the UI.

elements.description: Map of element-id -> element definition for this screen. Must be non-empty; the layout tree references these ids by key.

layout.description: The base layout tree for this screen (a recursive node). Arranges the screen's elements into splits, grids or presets.

variants.description: Optional per-device-class layout overrides. When the target device class matches a variant's class, its layout replaces the base layout.

class.description: Device class id this variant applies to (must match a class in the device manifest).

layout.description: Layout tree used when rendering on the named device class.

element.description: A single renderable widget (numeric value, gauge, compass, button, …). Its `type` selects the widget; `bindings` wire its fields to data sources.

type.description: Element kind, e.g. single-value, text, gauge, bar, trend, autopilot, compass, windrose, button. Known types have required bindings enforced by the semantic pass; unknown types are allowed but warned.

name.description: Optional label / title for the element.

bindings.description: Map of binding-field -> data source. E.g. `value` for the primary reading; compass/windrose also accept `dir`. Required bindings per type are checked semantically.

markers.description: Optional dial markers (for compass/windrose), each an object with an optional glyph.

action.description: Optional interaction performed when the element is activated (e.g. a button).

zoom.description: Optional zoom/detail level hint for the element.

source.description: A data source for a binding. The `kind` selects which other field is required: signalk->path, local->id, const->value, computed->expr.

kind.description: Source kind: `signalk` (a SignalK path), `local` (an onboard sensor id), `const` (a fixed value), or `computed` (an expression).

path.description: SignalK path, e.g. navigation.speedOverGround. Required when kind is `signalk`.

id.description: Onboard/local source id. Required when kind is `local`.

expr.description: Expression to evaluate. Required when kind is `computed`.

value.description: Literal value. Required when kind is `const`.

action.description: An interaction triggered by an element. `put` writes a value to a target; `nav` switches screen/target; `command` invokes a named command.

target.description: Action target (SignalK path, screen id, or command name depending on kind).

value.description: Value to write. Required when kind is `put`.

node.description: A recursive layout node. Exactly one of four shapes: an element leaf, a directional split (`flow` + `children`), a grid (`rows`/`cols`/`cells`), or a `preset` reference.

oneOf.description: Leaf node: places a single element (by its id in screen.elements) filling the available rect.

element.description: Element id (a key in the enclosing screen's `elements`).

oneOf.description: Directional split: arranges children along one axis. `flow: row` = horizontal main axis (children side by side); `flow: col` = vertical main axis (children stacked).

flow.description: Main axis of the split. `row` lays children out horizontally; `col` lays them out vertically.

children.description: Ordered child nodes laid out along the flow axis. Must be non-empty.

weights.description: Optional relative sizes along the flow axis, one positive number per child. When present its length must equal children length (checked semantically).

oneOf.description: Grid: arranges cells in a rows x cols matrix, filled row-major. `cells` length must equal rows*cols (checked semantically).

cells.description: Grid cells in row-major order; each is itself a node. Must be non-empty.

oneOf.description: Preset reference: expands a named layout template, filling its holes from `slots`. The preset name must be known (checked semantically).

preset.description: Name of a known layout preset (e.g. full, hero-split).

slots.description: Element ids that fill the preset's holes, in the order the preset consumes them.

alarm.description: A threshold alarm: fires at `level` with `message` when `source` crosses `lt`/`gt`. At least one of lt/gt must be present.

id.description: Unique alarm id (must be unique across the document).

source.description: Data source whose value is compared against the thresholds.

level.description: Severity raised when the alarm fires.

lt.description: Fire when the source value is less than this number.

gt.description: Fire when the source value is greater than this number.

message.description: Human-readable message shown when the alarm fires.
